name: Rethink Robotics
summary: Maker of the Baxter and Sawyer collaborative robots for factories shut down in 2018 and sold its assets after failing to hit commercial scale.
lesson: Breakthrough robotics hardware still needs a clear ROI case for factory buyers, not just novel technology.

name: Berkshire Grey
summary: Warehouse automation robotics firm went public via SPAC in 2021 at a rich valuation, then was taken private by SoftBank in 2023 at a fraction of that value amid heavy losses.
lesson: SPAC valuations built on future contracts can collapse quickly when hardware deployment and margins lag projections.

name: Katerra
summary: SoftBank-backed construction manufacturing giant that vertically integrated factories, design and building collapsed into bankruptcy in 2021 after years of mismanagement and losses.
lesson: Vertically integrating an entire industry's supply chain at once multiplies operational risk faster than capital can cover it.

name: Desktop Metal
summary: Metal 3D-printing manufacturing company went public via SPAC in 2020, suffered years of heavy losses and merged into rival Stratasys in 2025 amid severe financial distress.
lesson: Aggressive SPAC-era acquisition sprees to build a manufacturing platform can leave a company with unsustainable cash burn.

name: Velo3D
summary: Metal additive-manufacturing systems maker went public via SPAC in 2021 and filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2025 after persistent losses and customer concentration in aerospace.
lesson: Metal 3D printing requires enormous capital for R&D and service, and revenue concentration in a few large customers is fragile.

name: Convoy
summary: Digital freight-matching and supply chain trucking marketplace shut down abruptly in 2023 after a slowing freight market made it unable to raise further funding.
lesson: Marketplace-style logistics platforms are highly cyclical, and thin margins can't absorb a prolonged freight recession.

name: GE Digital / Predix
summary: GE's ambitious industrial IoT software platform, meant to make GE a top-10 software company, was drastically scaled back and largely divested after 2018 amid huge losses.
lesson: An industrial conglomerate cannot force software-company economics onto a platform built primarily to serve its own hardware ecosystem.

name: Nikola Corporation
summary: Electric/hydrogen truck manufacturer went public via a high-profile SPAC merger in 2020, its founder was later convicted of fraud, and the company filed for bankruptcy in 2025.
lesson: Manufacturing claims about production readiness must be independently verifiable, since fabricated demos eventually collapse under real production demands.

name: Starsky Robotics
summary: Autonomous trucking company aimed at long-haul freight supply chains shut down in 2020 after failing to raise a new funding round.
lesson: Autonomous freight technology took far longer to commercialize than early investor timelines assumed.

name: Fast Radius
summary: On-demand digital manufacturing and 3D-printing platform went public via SPAC in 2022 and filed for bankruptcy in late 2023 after failing to generate sustainable margins.
lesson: On-demand manufacturing marketplaces need a viable unit economics model well before chasing a public listing.

name: Bright Machines
summary: Software-defined manufacturing and robotics automation company underwent major layoffs and restructuring in 2023-2024 after failing to hit growth targets.
lesson: Selling flexible robotics-as-a-service to factories requires proving ROI fast enough to justify the capital needed to build the platform.

name: Tulip Interfaces
summary: No-code frontline operations platform for factories reached unicorn status by letting manufacturers build custom apps for shop-floor workflows without heavy IT involvement.
lesson: A no-code approach to factory software lowers adoption friction versus traditional, rigid MES implementations.

name: Plex Systems
summary: Cloud-native manufacturing ERP/MES pioneer was acquired by Rockwell Automation in 2021 for $2.22 billion, validating its long-term bet on cloud manufacturing software.
lesson: Being early to cloud-native manufacturing ERP paid off once large industrial automation companies wanted a software layer to acquire.

name: project44
summary: Real-time supply chain and freight visibility platform reached unicorn status by building a large network of carrier and shipper data integrations.
lesson: Visibility platforms become more valuable as more carriers and shippers join the network, creating durable defensibility.

name: Coupa
summary: Cloud procurement and business spend management software company went public in 2016 and was acquired by Thoma Bravo in 2023 for roughly $8 billion.
lesson: Procurement software with strong network effects across suppliers and buyers can command premium acquisition multiples.

name: Augury
summary: AI-based predictive maintenance company using acoustic and vibration sensors reached unicorn status serving major manufacturers like PepsiCo and Colgate-Palmolive.
lesson: Machine-health monitoring with clear, quantifiable downtime-avoidance ROI drives strong enterprise adoption.

name: Samsara
summary: Industrial IoT and fleet/operations sensor platform went public in 2021 and has grown into a large public company serving industrial, logistics and manufacturing customers.
lesson: Combining hardware sensors with a strong cloud analytics platform can scale into a large public software-plus-hardware company.

name: Zetwerk
summary: Manufacturing marketplace connecting brands with contract manufacturers reached unicorn status by digitizing custom manufacturing sourcing in India.
lesson: A marketplace model for custom manufacturing can scale rapidly when it removes friction from sourcing and quality control.

name: FourKites
summary: Supply chain visibility platform grew into a unicorn by aggregating real-time tracking data across carriers for large manufacturers and retailers.
lesson: Consolidating fragmented carrier tracking data into one platform solves a real, painful visibility gap for shippers.

name: Fetch Robotics
summary: Warehouse and manufacturing autonomous mobile robot maker was acquired by Zebra Technologies in 2021 for $305 million after building a robots-as-a-service model.
lesson: A robots-as-a-service pricing model lowered adoption barriers and made the company an attractive acquisition for an industrial automation player.
